Heatwave conditions persist across Telangana, temperatures touch 44°C
Heatwave conditions continued across Telangana on Wednesday, with temperatures touching 44 degrees Celsius in several districts. The IMD has warned that heatwave conditions may persist till June 20 and has also forecast thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across the State.
Hyderabad: Heatwave conditions continued to influence weather across Telangana, with maximum temperatures on Wednesday in several parts of northern and eastern districts ranging between 41 degrees Celsius and 44 degrees Celsius.
Several parts of Hyderabad, Rangareddy, Sangareddy and Yadadri Bhuvanagiri recorded maximum temperatures between 39 degrees Celsius and 42 degrees Celsius, according to data from the Telangana State Development Planning Society (TGDPS).
A heatwave warning was issued for Adilabad, Kumram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem and Khammam districts.
The maximum temperatures on Wednesday ranged between 43 degrees Celsius and 44 degrees Celsius in Suryapet, Khammam, Kumram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nalgonda, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Hanumakonda, Peddapalli and Nizamabad, TGDPS data indicated.
Jagtial, Warangal, Nirmal, Kamareddy and Siddipet witnessed temperatures ranging between 42 degrees Celsius and 43 degrees Celsius.
A few areas in Hyderabad witnessed the highest temperatures ranging between 40 degrees Celsius and 41 degrees Celsius, while maximum temperatures in the remaining areas stayed below 40 degrees Celsius.
The IMD, Hyderabad, has also forecast thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds in almost all districts till June 22.

title: Telangana reels under severe heatwave temperatures cross 42 degrees - Newsonair
full_text:
Telangana is reeling under a severe heatwave, with temperatures continuing to rise across the state. Nizamabad recorded a high of 42.4 degrees Celsius, while Adilabad and Mahabubnagar also reported temperatures above 41 degrees. Even Hyderabad crossed the 40-degree mark, with most areas witnessing temperatures 2 to 3 degrees above normal.
Amid the worsening conditions, the government has urged people to take precautions, advising to avoid stepping out between 11 AM and 3 PM. Those going outdoors have been asked to use protective gear such as hats or headscarves to prevent heat-related illnesses.
The state government has directed officials to set up drinking water stations across Gram Panchayats and mandal centres to provide relief.
The Met Department has forecast that the heatwave will persist over the next few days.
